# Batch Tracking

## 3.1 Batch Record

**FR-ADVINV-009** — When an item is configured as batch-tracked, the system shall require batch number entry on every inbound stock movement (GRN, production receipt, stock adjustment positive); the system shall reject a batch-tracked GRN line that has no batch number.

**FR-ADVINV-010** — Each batch record shall carry: batch number (supplied by the user or generated by the system in format `BATCH-YYYY-NNNN`), manufacturing date, best-before date, expiry date, supplier batch reference (for purchased goods), and QC status (Released, Quarantined, Rejected).

**FR-ADVINV-011** — When a batch QC status is set to "Quarantined", the system shall block all outbound movements (sales picking, production issue, transfer) of that batch until the status is changed to "Released" by an authorised user.

## 3.2 Batch Ledger

**FR-ADVINV-012** — The system shall maintain a batch ledger for each batch record, recording every stock movement in and out with: movement date, movement type, quantity, document reference (GRN number, delivery note number, production order number), and resulting batch balance; the batch ledger shall be immutable.

**FR-ADVINV-013** — When a batch is fully consumed (batch balance = 0), the system shall close the batch record and retain it in read-only state for the configured retention period (minimum 7 years for food and pharmaceutical tenants).

## 3.3 Uganda Agro-Processing Batch Fields

**FR-ADVINV-014** — For items in the maize, grain, and coffee categories, the system shall extend the batch record with the following agro-processing fields: intake season, moisture content at intake (%), bulk density (kg/m³), grade (A, B, C or configurable), and originating cooperative or farmer group.

**FR-ADVINV-015** — When a batch record carries a cooperative intake reference, the system shall provide a forward trace report: given a batch number, show every delivery note and customer that received stock from that batch.
